name: Statue of Liberty
funFact: The statue's face was modeled after the sculptor's mother, Charlotte.

name: Sydney Opera House
funFact: The building was designed by Danish architect Jørn Utzon, who won a design competition in 1957.

name: Great Pyramid of Giza
funFact: It is the oldest of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World and the only one to remain largely intact.

name: Taj Mahal
funFact: The structure appears to change color depending on the time of day and the moon.

name: Machu Picchu
funFact: The Incas used a technique called ashlar, where stones are cut to fit together perfectly without mortar.

name: Mount Everest
funFact: Everest's height actually increases by about 4 millimeters every year due to tectonic plate movement.

name: Christ the Redeemer
funFact: The statue is frequently struck by lightning, occurring about 3-6 times a year.

name: The Colosseum
funFact: It is the largest ancient amphitheatre ever built, and is still the largest standing amphitheatre in the world.

name: Mount Fuji
funFact: Fuji is actually three separate volcanoes piled one on top of the other.

name: Easter Island (Moai)
funFact: The statues are known as Moai and were carved by the Rapa Nui people between 1250 and 1500.

name: Table Mountain
funFact: It is one of the oldest mountains in the world, over six times older than the Himalayas.

name: The London Eye
funFact: It has 32 capsules, representing the 32 boroughs of London, but they are numbered 1 to 33 (skipping 13).

name: Mount Kilimanjaro
funFact: It is the highest free-standing mountain in the world, meaning it is not part of a mountain range.

name: Sagrada Família
funFact: Construction began in 1882 and it is still not finished, surviving the Spanish Civil War.

name: Grand Canyon
funFact: The canyon is around 6 million years old, but some of the rocks at the bottom are 2 billion years old.

name: Petra
funFact: Petra is often called the 'Rose City' because of the color of the stone from which it is carved.

name: Angkor Wat
funFact: Originally built as a Hindu temple, it was gradually transformed into a Buddhist temple in the 12th century.

name: Galápagos Islands
funFact: Charles Darwin's visit here in 1835 inspired his theory of evolution by natural selection.

name: Burj Khalifa
funFact: It is so tall that you can watch the sunset from the base, then take the elevator to the top and watch it again.

name: The Acropolis
funFact: The Parthenon was actually used as a gunpowder magazine during the 17th century.
